text:
First, note that in 1795, Japan used local mean time based on longitude (approximately UTC+9). Asia/Tokyo time zone as we know it (JST = UTC+9) was not formally adopted until much later, but for historical consistency in such problems, we assume the local time corresponds to the modern fixed offset of UTC+9.

Given: October 23, 1795, 09:00 in Asia/Tokyo (UTC+9).

UTC = local time minus offset:  
09:00 − 9 hours = 00:00 (midnight) on the same date.

FINAL_ANSWER: 1795-10-23 00:00
